title: pow: core/src/pow.rs — token codec, hash, verify, mine
description:
New module core/src/pow.rs implementing the complete client+server PoW protocol.

What: Token codec, double-SHA256 computation, server-side verification, client-side mining.

Token format (31 bytes total):
  Offset  Field      Type   Size  Encoding
  0       ver        u8     1     0x01
  1       op_type    u8     1     0x01 (enrollment)
  2-5     ts_floor   u32    4     big-endian (Unix_ts / 60)
  6       diff       u8     1     leading zero bits (default 20)
  7-14    nonce      u64    8     big-endian (mined value)
  15-30   h16        [u8;16] 16   first 16 bytes of double-SHA256(hash_input)

Hash input (111 bytes):
  ver(1) || op_type(1) || ts_floor_be32(4) || diff(1) || hex_lower(pub_key_32_bytes)(64) || server_salt(32) || nonce_be64(8)

Algorithm for compute_hash(ver, op_type, ts_floor, diff, pub_key_bytes, server_salt, nonce):
  1. Build payload: concatenate all fields per spec above
     - hex_lower: format each byte as {:02x}
  2. hash1 = SHA256(payload)
  3. hash2 = SHA256(hash1)
  4. return hash2 as [u8; 32]

Algorithm for verify_token(token_b64url, pub_key_bytes, server_salt, now_unix_secs, min_diff):
  1. Decode token from base64url; must be exactly 31 bytes
  2. Extract fields per offset table above
  3. Reject if ver != 0x01
  4. Reject if diff < min_diff (default 20) — prevents difficulty downgrade attacks
  5. Reject if diff > 30 — prevents impossible challenges
  6. Compute ts_floor_now = now_unix_secs / 60
     delta = ts_floor_now.abs_diff(token.ts_floor as u64)
     Reject if delta > 10 — staleness check (±600s)
  7. Reconstruct h16: compute_hash(ver, op_type, ts_floor, diff, pub_key_bytes, server_salt, nonce)
     Compare first 16 bytes to token.h16; reject if mismatch (this also validates leading zeros)
  8. Count leading zero bits of compute_hash output; reject if < diff
  9. Return Ok(token.h16) — caller adds h16 to replay set

Algorithm for mine_token(pub_key_bytes, server_salt, diff, ts_floor):
  1. nonce = 0u64
  2. loop:
     hash = compute_hash(1, 1, ts_floor, diff, pub_key_bytes, server_salt, nonce)
     if count_leading_zero_bits(hash) >= diff: break
     nonce += 1
  3. Build 31-byte token struct
  4. Encode to base64url (URL_SAFE_NO_PAD engine)
  5. Return token string

count_leading_zero_bits([u8; 32]):
  sum of 8 for each leading 0x00 byte, then leading_zeros() of first non-zero byte

Expose from lib.rs: pub mod pow;

Defensive programming:
- Never unwrap base64 decode; return Err
- server_salt must never appear in any log or error string
- diff bounds: 20 <= diff <= 30; reject outside range
- Use URL_SAFE_NO_PAD base64 engine for token encoding (base64::engine::general_purpose::URL_SAFE_NO_PAD)
- ts_floor is u32; use wrapping_add in delta check to avoid overflow
- pub_key_bytes is 32 bytes from Ed25519 verifying key; panic-guard with assert_eq!(len, 32)

Tests (with independent oracles — NOT derived from code under test):
- test_token_roundtrip: encode then decode Token struct round-trips correctly
- test_difficulty_1: construct a hash input with known bytes and diff=1; manually verify first bit is zero
- test_verify_rejects_wrong_diff: token with diff=5 but relay expects min_diff=20 → Err
- test_verify_rejects_stale: token with ts_floor 11+ minutes ago → Err  
- test_verify_rejects_bad_h16: corrupt token.h16 → Err
- test_leading_zeros: helper function unit test with known byte patterns (0x00=8, 0x80=0, 0x40=1, 0x7F=1, 0x0F=4)
- test_mine_roundtrip: mine with diff=1; verify resulting token passes verify_token
acceptance_criteria: cargo test -p nie-core passes; pow.rs exists with Token struct, compute_hash, verify_token, mine_token; all tests pass
close_reason: core/src/pow.rs: PowError, compute_hash, verify_token, mine_token, 6 unit tests
